title: WOS API Workflow Process
description: Public API resource schema for WOS workflow processes. Greenfield per ADR 0082 D-15 step 4. The base `WorkflowProcess` resource carries identity, workflow binding, lifecycle posture, current configuration, and `caseState` only; governance, tasks, timers, holds, and related cross-case links are subresources (D-3). The `?include=` aggregation seam exposes a closed taxonomy of subresources for single-call UI views without re-introducing GraphQL-style open composition. The kernel anti-design `caseStateMutations: Record<string, unknown>` is structurally inexpressible: state-mutation events are projected through `EvaluationResult.mutations: CaseStateMutation[]` with closed `mutationKind` and `verificationLevel` taxonomies (D-6) drawn from `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/MutationSource` and `#/$defs/VerificationLevel`.

ActorRef.description: Stable actor reference from the identity/governance subsystem (ADR 0082 D-9). Hoisted to `_common.schema.json` per PLN-0404.

WosResourceUrn.description: Public WOS API resource URN (ADR 0082 D-4). Hoisted to `_common.schema.json` per PLN-0404.

WorkflowUrl.description: Absolute URI of the governing WOS Workflow Document. Together with `workflowVersion` pins the exact author-time document controlling this instance's lifecycle, case-state schema, and contract bindings.

ImpactLevel.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension kernel impact-level taxonomy (Kernel S6) cross-`$ref`d from `task.schema.json` per ADR 0082 D-14 (`$ref` kernel/governance types from existing schemas instead of redefining). The kernel makes `impactLevel` REQUIRED on every Workflow Document (`wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/ImpactLevel`); this projection surfaces the value at instance scope so clients can branch on rights-/safety-impacting cases without re-fetching the workflow.

OutcomeCode.description: Machine-readable terminal-outcome marker populated only when `lifecycleState == completed` (Kernel S6 final-state `outcomeCode`). Identifier-shaped so downstream systems can branch on completion semantics without parsing state names. Absent on non-completed cases.

MilestoneId.description: Stable milestone identifier from the workflow document (Kernel S4 milestones; kernel:661-672). Sorted lexicographically when surfaced as a set.

ExpirableTimestamp.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp or the literal `never` sentinel for indefinite-duration semantics (ADR 0082 D-10). Used by continuation-of-services exits and similar policy-bounded windows.

DcrConstraintZoneState.description: Per-instance state of one DCR constraint zone declared on a `compound` lifecycle state (advanced/advanced-governance.md §1.2; advanced-governance.md:116-176). Carries the zone identifier, current escalation level (none → caution → breach), and the most recent trigger timestamp. Companion: Agent B's `dcrZoneViolation` Facts record kind on `provenance.schema.json` carries the per-event detail; this $def is the live state projection on `WorkflowProcess`.

zoneId.description: Stable DCR-zone identifier from the workflow's advanced-governance constraint-zone declaration.

currentLevel.description: Closed escalation level of the zone. `none` is nominal; `caution` is a warning posture (constraint approaching breach threshold); `breach` is an active constraint violation. Closed-no-extension by design.

lastTriggered.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp the zone last transitioned out of `none`. Absent when the zone has never triggered.

pendingActivities.description: Activity identifiers currently in the 'pending' marking within this DCR zone.

violatedRelations.description: DCR relations currently in violation within this zone.

relationType.description: Closed DCR relation type per advanced-governance.md.

source.description: Identifier of the constraint zone whose relation was violated.

target.description: Identifier of the constraint zone or actor that is the target of the violated relation.

LifecycleState.description: Closed instance lifecycle posture (ADR 0082 D-12). Byte-aligned mirror of the kernel `InstanceStatus` enum at `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/InstanceStatus` — itself the name-promoted alias of the inline `status` field on `wos-process.schema.json` (ADR 0082 D-13 gate 6 enforces this byte-parity). Closed-no-extension by design: `active` accepts events normally; `suspended` is paused; `migrating` is mid-definition-version-change; `completed` reached a top-level final state; `terminated` was explicitly terminated; `stalled` exhausted the commit retry budget (ADR 0070 D-5). The kernel taxonomy is closed; if a deployment needs new lifecycle postures, the kernel grows them and the API mirror tracks.

ConfigurationStateId.description: Single active leaf-state identifier in the kernel lifecycle hierarchy (Kernel S4.2). Dot-separated path of the leaf state within the lifecycle (for example `review.initial`).

CaseStateValue.description: Current case-file field values, carried as an opaque JSON value (typically an object whose keys are case-file field names declared in the governing Workflow Document's `caseFile.fields` per Kernel S5.2). The case-file shape is workflow-defined, not API-defined: per-workflow structural validation is the reference server's responsibility against the resolved Workflow Document; this contract treats the value as opaque-shaped. Mutation projection is via `CaseStateMutation`, whose `mutationKind` and `verificationLevel` taxonomies are closed (ADR 0082 D-6).

CompensationLogEntry.description: Single entry in the kernel compensation log (Kernel §9.5). Appended when the processor initiates a compensation sequence after a partial correlation-group fan-out failure or an explicit revert action. Read-only at the public API layer.

compensationId.description: Processor-assigned compensation identifier within this instance.

status.description: Closed compensation lifecycle. pending: queued but not started. executing: revert steps in progress. succeeded: all revert steps completed. failed: one or more revert steps could not be applied.

pivotStep.description: Identifier of the pivot step — the transition or mutation whose rollback triggered this compensation.

revertSteps.description: Ordered identifiers of the revert steps to execute.

startedAt.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when compensation began.

completedAt.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when compensation reached a terminal state (succeeded or failed). Absent while status is pending or executing.

items.description: Compensation log entries in this page.

cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available.

MutationKind.description: Origin of a case-state mutation (ADR 0082 D-6). Byte-aligned mirror of the kernel `MutationSource` open enum at `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/MutationSource` (ADR 0082 D-13 gate 6). Reserved literals: `agent-extracted` (an agent extracted the value); `system-fetched` (from an external system of record); `human-entered` (direct user entry); `human-corrected` (a corrective edit on a prior value); `computed` (from a derivation expression); `self-attested` (from an applicant assertion). Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix per the kernel pattern.

VerificationLevel.description: Verification strength of a case-state mutation (ADR 0082 D-6). Byte-aligned mirror of the kernel `VerificationLevel` open enum at `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/VerificationLevel` (ADR 0082 D-13 gate 6). Reserved literals: `independent` (corroborated by an independent source); `attested` (signed attestation by a named actor); `corroborated` (consistent with at least one other recorded source); `authoritative` (carried by an authoritative source of record). Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix per the kernel pattern.

CaseStateMutation.description: Single field-level case-state mutation event (ADR 0082 D-6). Replaces the explicit anti-design `caseStateMutations: Record<string, unknown>` from the prior `case-portal/src/ports/types.ts`: the wire shape requires `fieldPath`, `newValue`, `mutationKind`, and `verificationLevel`, so an open-taxonomy mutation bag is structurally inexpressible.

fieldPath.description: JSON-pointer or dotted path identifying the case-file field this mutation writes (Kernel S5.4).

previousValue.description: Value at this `fieldPath` immediately before the mutation. Any JSON value, including `null`. Omitted when the field had no prior value (first write).

newValue.description: Value at this `fieldPath` immediately after the mutation. Any JSON value, including `null` for an explicit erasure marker.

actorRef.description: Actor URN that authored or attested the mutation. OPTIONAL when the runtime cannot canonically resolve a single actor (for example a `computed` mutation derived purely from prior case state).

timestamp.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the mutation was applied (ADR 0069).

EvaluationOutcomeEventName.description: Name of an event that fired during the evaluation. Reserved kernel events follow the `$`-prefix convention (Kernel S4.10); document-authored events are bare identifiers.

EvaluationResult.description: Result of evaluating a single event against an instance (ADR 0082 D-6). Carries the lifecycle configuration before and after the evaluation, the events that fired during the evaluation, and a typed array of case-state mutations. The kernel anti-design `caseStateMutations: Record<string, unknown>` is replaced by `mutations: CaseStateMutation[]`; the closed `mutationKind` and `verificationLevel` taxonomies make the open-bag form structurally inexpressible.

previousConfiguration.description: Active leaf-state configuration immediately before the evaluation.

newConfiguration.description: Active leaf-state configuration immediately after the evaluation.

eventsFired.description: Events that fired during the evaluation, in evaluation order.

mutations.description: Typed array of case-state mutations applied during the evaluation. Empty when no mutations occurred.

WorkflowProcess.description: Public workflow-process resource (ADR 0082 D-3). Carries identity, workflow binding, lifecycle posture, current configuration, `impactLevel` (Kernel S6 proportionality index), and `caseState` only; subresources are accessed via dedicated endpoints or the closed `?include=` aggregation seam (`WorkflowProcessWithIncludes`). Mirrors structural fields from the kernel runtime artifact `wos-process.schema.json` but is a wire projection: identifiers are URNs (D-4), `lifecycleState` is the closed posture taxonomy (not the kernel raw `status`), and subresource fields (`activeTasks`, `timers`, `holds`, etc.) are intentionally absent.

id.description: Stable public URN for this runtime process projection. In the case/process route surface this is the canonical runtime-process identity; the durable case ledger is carried separately by `caseLedgerId`.

caseLedgerId.description: Durable governed case-ledger URN (`urn:wos:<tenant>_case_<ulid>`). Multiple workflow runtime processes may append to the same case ledger.

processId.description: Workflow runtime process URN (`urn:wos:<tenant>_process_<ulid>`) that owns this live execution projection. Direct case-ledger appends omit process identity; `WorkflowProcess` resources always carry it.

workflowUrl.description: Absolute URI of the governing WOS Workflow Document.

workflowVersion.description: Version of the Workflow Document pinned at instance creation (Kernel S9.6). Running instances remain on their creation-time version unless explicitly migrated.

lifecycleState.description: Closed lifecycle posture for this instance.

impactLevel.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension kernel impact level (Kernel S6; kernel:826) projected from the governing Workflow Document. REQUIRED — the kernel makes `impactLevel` REQUIRED on every Workflow Document and uses it as a proportionality index for governance, AI-oversight, and review-protocol gating; surfacing it on the case projection lets clients branch on `rights-impacting` / `safety-impacting` cases without re-fetching the workflow.

configuration.description: Ordered active leaf-state configuration at the time of this projection (Kernel S4.2). Empty for terminal instances.

stalledSince.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp recording when the instance entered `stalled` posture (ADR 0070 D-5). REQUIRED on the wire whenever `lifecycleState == stalled`; absent otherwise.

outcomeCode.description: Machine-readable terminal-outcome marker. Populated only when `lifecycleState == completed` (Kernel S6 final-state `outcomeCode`); absent on every other lifecycle state.

milestonesFired.description: Set of milestone identifiers fired on this case so far (Kernel S4 milestones; kernel:661-672). Sorted lexicographically. Companion: Agent B's `milestoneFired` literal on `FactsRecordKind` carries the per-event detail; this field is the cumulative set on the live instance projection.

continuationOfServicesActive.description: True when the case is operating under a continuation-of-services policy (governance §3.6 — `AppealMechanism.continuationOfServices`; workflow-governance.md:154-158). Surfaces the existence of the policy without leaking the underlying policy rationale; the staff caseworker uses this to avoid premature termination during an active appeal window.

continuationOfServicesEndsAt.description: Absolute UTC RFC 3339 timestamp at which the active continuation-of-services window closes, or the literal `never` for indefinite continuation (governance §3.6). Present only when `continuationOfServicesActive == true`.

dcrZones.description: Per-instance DCR constraint-zone state (advanced/advanced-governance.md §1.2). Empty when the workflow declares no constraint zones or none have transitioned out of `none`. Cross-cite Agent B's `dcrZoneViolation` Facts record kind for the per-event detail in provenance.

caseState.description: Authoritative case-file snapshot at this projection point (Kernel S5).

createdAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the instance was created.

updatedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the projection was last refreshed.

tenant.description: Deployment tenant for this workflow process (ADR 0068 D-1.1). Single DNS label: lowercase ASCII letter first, then lowercase letters, digits, or hyphen, length 1 to 63.

correlationKey.description: Cross-case correlation identifier used by the kernel to route events to related instances (Kernel S9.4). OPTIONAL; absent when no correlation is in effect.

CaseLedgerEventSummary.description: Latest direct case-ledger event summary for `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}`. This summarizes the durable case provenance chain keyed by the case ledger, not workflow-process provenance rows.

eventId.description: Storage-local provenance row identifier for the direct case-ledger event.

event.description: Registered WOS event literal carried by the provenance payload when present. This is the authoritative D26 dispatch value; the case-ledger projection does not expose the redundant inner `recordKind` discriminator.

sequence.description: Monotonic sequence number within the direct case-ledger provenance chain.

eventHash.description: Hash-chain digest for this direct case-ledger event.

previousHash.description: Hash-chain predecessor digest for this direct case-ledger event.

timestamp.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the event row was appended.

CaseLedgerProjection.description: Durable case-ledger projection returned by `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}`. Unlike `WorkflowProcess`, this resource is keyed by the governed case ledger and may have zero workflow processes when the case was created through the direct genesis append surface.

eventCount.description: Count of direct case-ledger provenance events keyed by this case ledger. Process-routed provenance remains attached to each workflow process.

latestEvent.description: Latest direct case-ledger event when the case has a direct provenance chain. Absent for process-only cases.

processCount.description: Number of workflow runtime processes currently bound to this durable case ledger.

processes.description: Workflow process projections bound to this case ledger. Empty for a direct case-ledger genesis with no process started yet.

DelegationEntry.description: Single delegation record visible on a workflow process (governance S11). Mirrors the WOS governance `Delegation` model; full details live on the `governance` domain when it ratifies (ADR 0082 D-15 step 5). This shape is the minimum projection needed by the case view.

authority.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension delegation authority kind (governance S11.2). Vendor extensions MUST use an `x-` prefix.

effectiveDate.description: ISO 8601 date the delegation becomes active. Omitted when not date-bounded.

expirationDate.description: ISO 8601 date the delegation expires, or the literal `never` for indefinite delegations (ADR 0082 D-10 sentinel pattern).

revoked.description: True when the delegation has been revoked. Revoked delegations remain visible for audit but do not authorize new actions.

ReviewState.description: Closed review-protocol posture for a workflow process (governance S4). Reserved literals: `none` (no review attached); `pending` (review required but not started); `in-review` (review actively underway); `cleared` (review completed and approved); `flagged` (review identified an issue requiring resolution). Vendor extensions MUST use an `x-` prefix.

WorkflowProcessGovernance.description: Governance subresource for a workflow process (ADR 0082 D-3). Returns the active delegation set and the current review-protocol posture. Full delegation/review records live on the dedicated `governance` domain when it ratifies.

processId.description: Workflow process this governance projection belongs to.

delegations.description: Delegations currently in effect for this instance, ordered by `effectiveDate` ascending.

reviewState.description: Closed review-protocol posture for this instance.

adverseDecisionPolicyActive.description: When true, the workflow's adverse-decision policy is active for this case. The case portal SHOULD surface notices about appeal rights, deadlines, and continuation-of-services windows.

reviewProtocolActive.description: The review protocol currently active on this workflow process. Projected from the workflow's review-protocol declaration. Present only when a review-protocol-governed transition is in the active configuration.

activeEscalation.description: Current escalation posture for this instance. Present only when an escalation chain is actively walking. Absent when the instance is not escalated.

escalatedTo.description: Actor URN the case or task was reassigned to.

escalatedAt.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when this escalation level fired.

activeHoldsCount.description: Count of currently active holds on this instance. The holds subresource carries the per-hold detail.

activeGovernanceRules.description: Governance rules currently active on this instance's state configuration.

ruleId.description: Stable identifier of the governance rule that is currently active on this instance.

triggerTag.description: Semantic tag that triggered activation of this governance rule.

activatedAt.description: RFC 3339 timestamp recording when this governance rule became active.

TimerEntry.description: Single durable timer projection for a workflow process. Mirrors `wos-process.schema.json#/$defs/TimerState`; `event` retains the kernel raw event name without the API-layer URN rewrite because timers fire kernel events, not API resources.

timerId.description: Stable processor-assigned timer identifier within this instance.

deadline.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp at which this timer fires.

event.description: Kernel event name dispatched when the timer fires. Typically a `$timeout.*` kernel-generated event (Kernel S4.10).

scopeState.description: Lifecycle state at which this timer is scoped. The timer cancels automatically when the scope state is exited (Kernel S9.7).

WorkflowProcessTimerList.description: Timers subresource for a workflow process (ADR 0082 D-3). Returns the durable timer set; this is a small, bounded set per instance, so the response is not cursor-paginated.

processId.description: Workflow process these timers belong to.

HoldEntry.description: Single typed hold projection on a workflow process (governance S12). Carries the hold reason, expected duration (or the literal `indefinite` for legal-hold), and the resume trigger.

holdType.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension hold reason (governance S12.2). Vendor extensions MUST use an `x-` prefix.

expectedDuration.description: ISO 8601 duration (with the WOS `P<N>BD` business-day extension), or the literal `indefinite` for `legal-hold` policies.

resumeTrigger.description: Kernel event name that resumes the case from this hold. Omitted when `holdType == legal-hold`.

engagedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the hold was engaged.

expectedReleaseAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the hold's expected duration elapses. Omitted when `expectedDuration == indefinite`.

WorkflowProcessHoldList.description: Holds subresource for a workflow process (ADR 0082 D-3). Returns active holds; small, bounded set per instance, so not cursor-paginated.

processId.description: Workflow process these holds belong to.

RelatedCaseLink.description: Single cross-case related-instance link (Kernel S5.5; kernel:775). Lets clients navigate from a case to its parent appeal, sibling household members, replaced predecessor, or freely-labelled `related`/`supersedes` peer per kernel reservations.

relationship.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension relationship kind. Reserved literals reconcile two prior taxonomies: kernel S5.5 reserves `related` and `supersedes`; the API previously carried `predecessor|successor|appeals|appealed-by` plus the family triplet `parent|child|sibling`. The merged set is now authoritative. Vendor extensions MUST use an `x-` prefix.

bidirectional.description: When true, the relationship is symmetric — a corresponding `RelatedCaseLink` exists on the peer case pointing back at this one (Kernel S5.5; kernel:775). Defaults to `false` when absent: directional links (`predecessor → successor`, `appeals → appealed-by`) are the common case; symmetric kinds like `sibling`/`related` typically set this `true`. The default is implicit-`false`.

semanticLabel.description: Free-form prose label disambiguating the relationship (Kernel S5.5; kernel:775). Open prose — rendering concern, not contract; useful when several `related` links exist on the same case and the operator needs to tell them apart in a UI.

establishedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the relationship was established.

WorkflowProcessRelatedList.description: Related-cases subresource for a workflow process (ADR 0082 D-3). Returns the cross-case link set; not cursor-paginated because related-case sets are small.

processId.description: Workflow process these links originate from.

IncludeKind.description: Closed `?include=` aggregation seam for `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}` (ADR 0082 D-3). No vendor extensions: the aggregation surface is closed by design so server cost is bounded and the GraphQL-style open composition the platform thesis rejects stays inexpressible.

WorkflowProcessWithIncludes.description: Aggregated single-instance response for `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}?include=...` (ADR 0082 D-3). The `instance` envelope is the same `WorkflowProcess` shape that lives at `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}` with no `?include=`; subresource fields appear only when their literal is present in the query parameter. Per-section limits (for example `tasks(limit=10)`) are enforced server-side; the inline `tasks` shape projects `TaskListItem` from `task.schema.json` so list views inside the aggregation match the standalone task list.

compensation.description: Compensation log subresource. Present only when `include=compensation` was requested. Returns cursor-paginated entries; clients consume the full log via `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/compensation` for pagination.

governance.description: Governance subresource. Present only when `include=governance` was requested.

tasks.description: Task list projection (lighter `TaskListItem` shape from `task.schema.json`). Present only when `include=tasks` was requested. Capped per-section by the server; clients consume the full task list via `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/tasks` for cursor pagination.

timers.description: Timers subresource. Present only when `include=timers` was requested.

holds.description: Holds subresource. Present only when `include=holds` was requested.

related.description: Related-cases subresource. Present only when `include=related` was requested.

WorkflowProcessListOptions.description: Reserved directory-style workflow-process list query fields (ADR 0082 D-7). The reference server's case-scoped `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes` route currently returns an unpaginated `WorkflowProcessList` and does not consume this shape.

lifecycleState.description: Filter to instances in the supplied lifecycle posture. Repeating the query parameter narrows to a posture set.

workflowUrl.description: Filter to instances of the supplied Workflow Document URL.

createdAfter.description: Inclusive UTC RFC 3339 lower bound on `createdAt` (ADR 0069).

createdBefore.description: Exclusive UTC RFC 3339 upper bound on `createdAt`.

tenant.description: Tenant scope filter (ADR 0068 D-1.1). MUST agree with the `X-WOS-Tenant` header when both are present; the server rejects mismatches with `WOS-1422`.

include.description: Closed list of subresources to embed (`?include=...`). Repeated query parameter or comma-separated form. Per-section limits are enforced server-side.

cursor.description: Opaque resume token returned by the previous page (ADR 0082 D-7).

limit.description: Maximum number of instances requested for this page.

cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.

WorkflowProcessList.description: Unpaginated list of workflow-process projections bound to one durable case ledger. This is the wire response for `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes` in the reference server; cursor-paginated process directory browsing remains out of scope for the case-scoped subresource.

WorkflowProcessCreateRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes` in the reference server. Servers create or accept the process identifier, bind it to the durable case ledger, pin `definitionVersion` when supplied, and set the initial `caseState` from `initialCaseState` when present.

definitionUrl.description: Absolute URI of the WOS Workflow Document this process is created against.

definitionVersion.description: Optional pinned Workflow Document version for this process.

processId.description: Optional caller-supplied workflow process TypeID or WOS process URN. When omitted, the server allocates a new process TypeID for the supplied case ledger.

tenant.description: Explicit deployment tenant for this instance (ADR 0068 D-1.1). When omitted, the server resolves it from `X-WOS-Tenant`.

initialCaseState.description: Optional initial case-state object applied to the newly bound process.

IntakeHandoffOutcome.description: Closed-no-extension outcome of `acceptIntakeHandoff` policy evaluation (ADR 0073 D-7; Kernel S11.4.1 step 5). Reserved literals: `accepted` -- the handoff was accepted and a new governed case was born (corresponds to the kernel `accepted + createGovernedCase` policy decision and emits the `intakeAccepted` + `caseCreated` Facts `recordKind` values); `attachToExistingCase` -- the handoff was accepted and attached to a pre-existing governed case (kernel `accepted + attachToExistingCase`; emits `intakeAccepted` only); `deferred` -- the handoff was received but withheld from governed-case mutation pending later host action (kernel `deferred`; emits `intakeDeferred`). Closed-no-extension because the kernel-defined acceptance vocabulary is normative; vendor extension would create rival case-truth semantics (ADR 0073 D-1).

WorkflowProcessCreateResponse.description: Reserved intake-handoff creation response envelope (ADR 0073 D-7; Kernel S11.4). The reference server's case-scoped `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes` route returns `WorkflowProcess` directly; this envelope remains available for adapters that expose `acceptIntakeHandoff` policy outcomes as a separate contract.

outcome.description: Resolved acceptance policy decision per ADR 0073 D-7.

attachedToInstanceId.description: URN of the pre-existing governed-workflow process the handoff was attached to. REQUIRED when `outcome == attachToExistingCase`; absent otherwise. The `instance` field carries the full projection of that same case after attachment so clients can render the result without a follow-up GET.

deferralReason.description: Free-prose host-supplied reason for deferral. REQUIRED when `outcome == deferred`; absent otherwise. Used to render the operator-facing 'why' on the case-portal pending-intake list. Length-bounded to keep the response shape predictable.

EventSubmissionRequest.description: Request body for workflow input submission through `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/inputs` (Kernel S4.9 + S11.3 `processEvent`). Submits an authored event for evaluation against the running process; the server runs the kernel transition algorithm and returns the resulting `EvaluationResult` plus the post-evaluation `lifecycleState`. The `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header is REQUIRED (ADR 0082 D-16); a repeat request within the retention window returns the original `EventSubmissionResponse` unchanged. Submissions to a process whose `lifecycleState` is not `active` (Kernel S11.5) are rejected with `WOS-1409`.

eventName.description: Name of the event to deliver. Document-authored events are bare identifiers; kernel-reserved synthetic events (the `$`-prefix family per Kernel S4.10) MUST NOT be submitted by callers -- they are processor-emitted only and the server rejects them with `WOS-1422`.

payload.description: Optional event payload, carried as the same opaque-shaped JSON value used for `caseState`. Workflow-defined: validated by the server against the workflow's declared event-payload schema (when present, per Kernel S4.10) before evaluation; rejection is `WOS-1422`. Reusing `CaseStateValue` keeps the wire envelope closed at the API boundary while the per-payload shape stays workflow-driven.

actorRef.description: Actor URN authoring this event submission (ADR 0082 D-9). REQUIRED -- every mutation carries an actor for Facts-tier provenance (Kernel S3 + S8.2).

occurredAt.description: Optional client-asserted UTC RFC 3339 timestamp at which the originating real-world event occurred. The server records this verbatim on the resulting `stateTransition` record but uses its own clock for kernel time semantics (ADR 0069). Omitted when the caller has no out-of-band timing claim.

correlationKey.description: Optional cross-case correlation identifier the server uses to fan out related-instance events (Kernel S9.4). When omitted the server uses the instance's stored `correlationKey` from `WorkflowProcess.correlationKey`.

PerInstanceCorrelationResult.description: Per-instance result of a correlation-keyed event fan-out (Kernel S9.4). Records the outcome the server reached against one related instance when a single submitted event was fanned out across the correlation group. `status` is closed-no-extension because the fan-out outcome space is normative — the kernel either evaluated, refused, or skipped the related instance, and inventing a fourth outcome would create a rival case-truth semantics the platform thesis rejects.

processId.description: Related workflow process URN the server attempted to fan out to.

status.description: Closed-no-extension outcome of the per-instance evaluation. `succeeded` -- the server evaluated the event against this instance and committed the resulting transition; `failed` -- the server attempted the evaluation and the kernel refused (lifecycle posture, validation, or runtime error -- `failureCode` carries the WOS error code); `skipped` -- the server elected not to evaluate (e.g., the instance was outside the caller's scope per ADR 0068 or the kernel correlation predicate excluded it). New outcomes require a major schema bump.

failureCode.description: Stable WOS error code classifying the per-instance failure. REQUIRED when `status == failed`; absent on `succeeded` or `skipped`. Drawn from the public registry (`error-registry.md`) -- typically `WOS-1409` (lifecycle-posture mismatch) or `WOS-1422` (payload validation against the related instance's workflow event schema).

evaluatedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp at which the per-instance evaluation completed (ADR 0069). Omitted on `skipped` because no evaluation occurred. Drawn from the server clock.

CorrelationGroupResult.description: Per-event fan-out result envelope reported on `EventSubmissionResponse` when the submitted event triggered a correlation-group fan-out (Kernel S9.4). The server runs the kernel transition algorithm against each related instance independently and reports the typed per-instance outcome here -- best-effort-with-typed-failure semantics, NOT all-or-nothing. When `allSucceeded == false`, callers MUST inspect `perInstanceResults` to determine compensation. The shape makes silent partial-failure structurally impossible: every related instance the server attempted is named, and a missing entry is itself a contract bug.

correlationKey.description: Cross-case correlation identifier this fan-out group is keyed on (Kernel S9.4). Equals the resolved correlation key the server used -- either the request's explicit `correlationKey` or the instance's stored `WorkflowProcess.correlationKey` when the request omitted one.

perInstanceResults.description: Typed per-instance outcomes, one entry per related instance the server attempted to fan out to. Order follows the kernel's correlation-group enumeration order (deterministic per Kernel S9.4); the originating instance the request targeted MAY be included or omitted at server discretion -- callers MUST NOT depend on its presence here because `EventSubmissionResponse.evaluationResult` already carries that result.

allSucceeded.description: Convenience marker -- `true` when every entry in `perInstanceResults` has `status == succeeded`, `false` otherwise. When `false`, callers MUST inspect `perInstanceResults` for the per-instance failure surface to drive compensation (cross-cite ADR 0070 cross-layer failure and compensation when ratified).

CustodyReceipt.description: Trellis custody-anchoring receipt for a workflow process. Projected from the Trellis canonical-event-hash anchor receipt produced when the WOS processor appends provenance records through the custodyHook seam (Kernel §10.5). Read-only at the public API layer — the write path is internal (processor → Trellis via custodyHook).

processId.description: Workflow process URN this custody receipt anchors.

canonicalEventHash.description: Trellis canonical-event-hash text for the anchored event. Current Trellis service receipts use `sha256:<lowercase hex>`; older local fixtures may use bare lowercase hex. This is the cryptographic proof that the case's provenance records were anchored on Trellis — a verifier recomputes the hash from the provenance byte stream and compares against this value.

anchoredAt.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when the Trellis anchor was confirmed.

anchorTarget.description: Trellis anchor target URI (e.g. OpenTimestamps calendar URL, Rekor transparency log entry, Trillian Merkle tree path). Absent when the deployment uses an anchorless Trellis posture.

provenanceRecordId.description: Optional Facts-tier provenance record URN whose canonical event this receipt anchors. Absent when the receipt covers a batch of records.

custodyPosture.description: Trellis trust-profile label declared in the workflow's custody block (e.g. 'profile-b', 'lightweight'). Matches the trust-profile the custodyHook was configured with at append time.

EventSubmissionResponse.description: Response body for `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/inputs`. Wraps the kernel `EvaluationResult` (which carries `previousConfiguration`, `newConfiguration`, `eventsFired`, `mutations`) plus the post-evaluation lifecycle posture and the URN of the Facts-tier provenance record the evaluation produced. Clients drive the case-portal UI off the embedded `evaluationResult.mutations` typed array (ADR 0082 D-6) -- the kernel anti-design `caseStateMutations: Record<string, unknown>` is structurally inexpressible. When the submission triggered correlation-group fan-out (Kernel S9.4), `correlationGroupResult` carries the typed per-instance outcomes; absence means no fan-out occurred (single-instance evaluation only).

evaluationResult.description: Result of running the kernel transition algorithm against the supplied event.

newLifecycleState.description: Lifecycle posture after this evaluation. Equal to the prior state when the event was unmatched or the transition was within `active`; transitions to `completed` when the firing transition entered a top-level final state (Kernel S11.5).

evaluatedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp at which the kernel evaluation completed (ADR 0069). Drawn from the server clock, not from the caller's `occurredAt`.

provenanceRecordId.description: URN of the Facts-tier `stateTransition` (or `unmatchedEvent`) provenance record produced by this evaluation (Kernel S11.3). Clients can dereference it via `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/provenance` for full reasoning/counterfactual/narrative tier data (ADR 0082 D-5). Omitted only when the underlying processor cannot canonically resolve a single producer record (e.g., a no-op evaluation against a `completed` instance is rejected upstream with `WOS-1409` and never reaches this shape).

correlationGroupResult.description: Typed per-instance fan-out outcomes when the submission triggered a correlation-group fan-out (Kernel S9.4). Present only when fan-out occurred; absent when the event was evaluated against a single instance only. Best-effort-with-typed-failure semantics: when `allSucceeded == false`, callers MUST inspect `perInstanceResults` for compensation.

RuntimeDrainStep.description: Summary of one runtime drain step returned by `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/drain`.

processedEvent.description: Event name processed by this drain step, when a queued event was present.

transitionsCount.description: Number of workflow state transitions committed during this drain step.

provenanceCount.description: Number of provenance rows emitted during this drain step.

createdTaskIds.description: Task identifiers created during this drain step.

emittedEvents.description: Workflow event names emitted during this drain step.

RuntimeDrainStepList.description: Ordered runtime drain step summaries returned by `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/drain`.

SuspendInstanceRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/suspend` (Kernel S11.3 `suspend`). Sets `lifecycleState` to `suspended` and produces an `instanceSuspended` Facts-tier provenance record. The `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header is REQUIRED (ADR 0082 D-16); a repeat request within the retention window returns the original `WorkflowProcess` unchanged. Submissions to an instance whose `lifecycleState` is not `active` are rejected with `WOS-1409`.

reason.description: Operator-supplied prose reason for the suspension. Length-bounded to keep the wire envelope predictable; surfaced verbatim on the `instanceSuspended` Facts-tier provenance record.

actorRef.description: Actor URN authorizing the suspension (ADR 0082 D-9). REQUIRED -- every lifecycle-control mutation carries an actor.

holdUntil.description: Optional UTC RFC 3339 timestamp (or the `never` sentinel) at which the suspension is intended to lift. The server uses this to surface an expected resume time on `WorkflowProcessHoldList`; it does NOT auto-resume -- `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/resume` is still required (Kernel S11.3 `resume`). Omitted when no expected lift is known.

ResumeInstanceRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/resume` (Kernel S11.3 `resume`). Sets `lifecycleState` from `suspended` back to `active` and produces an `instanceResumed` Facts-tier provenance record. Pending events queued during the suspension are evaluated in delivery order after the status flip (Kernel S11.5). The `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header is REQUIRED (ADR 0082 D-16); a repeat request within the retention window returns the original `WorkflowProcess` unchanged. Submissions to an instance whose `lifecycleState` is not `suspended` are rejected with `WOS-1409`.

actorRef.description: Actor URN authorizing the resume (ADR 0082 D-9). REQUIRED.

justification.description: Optional operator-supplied prose recording why the suspension is being lifted. Length-bounded; surfaced verbatim on the `instanceResumed` Facts-tier provenance record. Omitted when no separate justification is needed beyond the actor identity.

TerminationKind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension classifier for an irreversible `terminate` operation (Kernel S11.3). Reserved literals: `policy-violation` (a governance check determined the case must end without completion); `applicant-withdrawn` (the applicant or authorized agent withdrew the underlying request); `duplicate` (the case was identified as a duplicate of another and the canonical instance carries the work); `error` (the case entered an unrecoverable error posture); `administrative` (operator-initiated closure for a reason outside the other literals). Vendor extensions MUST use an `x-` prefix per ADR 0082 D-12.

TerminateInstanceRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/terminate` (Kernel S11.3 `terminate`). Sets `lifecycleState` to `terminated` (irreversible per Kernel S11.5) and produces an `instanceTerminated` Facts-tier provenance record. The `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header is REQUIRED (ADR 0082 D-16); a repeat request within the retention window returns the original `WorkflowProcess` unchanged. Submissions to an instance whose `lifecycleState` is `completed` or already `terminated` are rejected with `WOS-1409`.

reason.description: Operator-supplied prose reason for the termination. Length-bounded; surfaced verbatim on the `instanceTerminated` Facts-tier provenance record. The `terminationKind` is the machine-readable classifier; `reason` is the free-prose explanation.

actorRef.description: Actor URN authorizing the termination (ADR 0082 D-9). REQUIRED.

ProcessLifecycleRequest.description: Optional request envelope for ADR 0093 dual-identity case/process lifecycle routes (`POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/suspend|resume|terminate`). These routes validate the case/process binding before applying the lifecycle mutation; the wire body carries only an optional operator rationale. The canonical process-scoped lifecycle contracts with full actor and idempotency requirements remain `SuspendInstanceRequest`, `ResumeInstanceRequest`, and `TerminateInstanceRequest` on the primary instance routes per Kernel S11.3.

reason.description: Optional operator-supplied prose rationale for the lifecycle transition. Length-bounded when present; surfaced on the corresponding Facts-tier provenance record when the deployment records one.

ProcessLifecycleResponse.description: Response envelope for ADR 0093 dual-identity case/process lifecycle routes after a successful suspend, resume, or terminate. Returns the stable process and case-ledger identifiers together with the resulting `lifecycleState`.

processId.description: Workflow process resource URN for the bound process.

caseLedgerId.description: Durable case-ledger resource URN the process is bound to.

lifecycleState.description: Instance lifecycle posture after the mutation commits.

FieldPath.description: Case-file field path used by migration-map entries (Kernel S5.4). Dotted or slash-pointer-shaped per workflow; constrained character set keeps the wire envelope predictable.

FieldRename.description: Single rename instruction inside a `MigrationMap.fieldRenames` array. Carried as a typed object instead of an open key-value map so D-12 closed-taxonomy discipline holds at the API boundary; the array is ordered, but the kernel migration algorithm applies renames as a set.

FieldDefault.description: Single default-value instruction inside a `MigrationMap.fieldDefaults` array. Replaces an open key-value map: each entry is a typed `{path, value}` pair where `value` is any JSON shape declared in the new Workflow Document's `caseFile.fields`. The wire envelope stays closed at the API boundary; the value-space is workflow-driven and validated server-side.

path.description: Case-file field path that receives the default.

value.description: Default value for the keyed field. Any JSON value, including `null` for an explicit null-marker. Shape is constrained by the new Workflow Document's `caseFile.fields` declaration (Kernel S5.2) and validated server-side; the wire envelope leaves the value opaque-shaped to keep the contract workflow-driven.

FieldCoercion.description: Single type-coercion instruction inside a `MigrationMap.fieldCoercions` array. The target type is a closed-no-extension JSON type taxonomy because the JSON type system is normatively closed.

path.description: Case-file field path whose value is coerced.

targetType.description: Closed-no-extension JSON type the existing value is coerced to before the new definition's validation runs. Coercion is best-effort; failure aborts the migration with `WOS-1422` and leaves the instance on its prior version (Kernel S11.2 atomicity).

MigrationMap.description: Case-state transformation map applied during instance migration (Kernel S11.2 step 2). Mirrors the kernel four-key bag (`fieldRenames | fieldRemovals | fieldDefaults | fieldCoercions`) but exposes each as a typed array of `{path, ...}` objects rather than open key-value maps so D-12 closed-taxonomy discipline holds at the API boundary. Every array is OPTIONAL -- an empty `MigrationMap` is valid and means 'no case-state transformation needed' (the new definition is structurally identical at the case-file level, only behavior changes). The server applies the four operations in fixed order: `fieldRemovals` -> `fieldRenames` -> `fieldDefaults` -> `fieldCoercions`.

fieldRenames.description: Renames applied after removals so a rename never targets a removed field. Order within the array is preserved on the wire but the migration algorithm applies them as a set (no rename may chain through another rename's `from`).

fieldRemovals.description: Field paths to remove from the case-file before the new definition's validation runs. Removed fields are NOT recoverable post-migration; provenance records the original values.

fieldDefaults.description: Default values for case-file fields newly introduced by the target definition. Applied after renames so a default never overwrites a value that was renamed into the path.

fieldCoercions.description: Type coercions applied last so the existing values (post-rename, post-default) match the new definition's `caseFile.fields` types.

MigrateInstanceRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/migrate` (Kernel S11.2 `migrate`; ADR 0071 D-2). Atomically updates the governing Workflow Document version and applies a case-state transformation map; on success produces an `instanceMigrated` Facts-tier record AND a `migrationPinChanged` Facts-tier record (ADR 0071 D-4 -- `migrationPinChanged` is the cross-layer provenance kind that anchors the chain transition for downstream verification). The `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header is REQUIRED (ADR 0082 D-16); a repeat request within the retention window returns the original `MigrationResult` unchanged. Migration is atomic: any step failure leaves the instance on its prior version (Kernel S11.2). Submissions to an instance whose `lifecycleState` is `completed` or `terminated` are rejected with `WOS-1409`; submissions whose target definition lacks a state currently in the configuration are rejected with `WOS-1422` (kernel `stateNotFound`).

targetDefinitionUrl.description: Optional absolute URI of a different Workflow Document to migrate to. Omitted when migrating to a newer version of the same `workflowUrl` (the common case). When supplied the server validates that the target document declares the same `correlationKey` family as the source.

targetDefinitionVersion.description: Version string of the target Workflow Document (Kernel S9.6). Pairs with the resolved `targetDefinitionUrl` (or the instance's existing `workflowUrl` when omitted) to pin the new governing document.

migrationMap.description: Case-state transformation bag (Kernel S11.2 step 2). MAY be the empty object when no case-state transformation is needed.

actorRef.description: Actor URN authorizing the migration (ADR 0082 D-9). REQUIRED.

justification.description: Operator-supplied prose justification for the migration. REQUIRED -- migration is a high-impact governance event under ADR 0071, and the audit trail demands a recorded 'why' alongside the actor identity. Surfaced verbatim on the `migrationPinChanged` Facts-tier record.

MigrationResult.description: Response body for `POST /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/migrate`. Carries the post-migration `WorkflowProcess` projection plus the URNs of the two Facts-tier provenance records produced by the operation (`instanceMigrated` per Kernel S11.3 and `migrationPinChanged` per ADR 0071 D-4). Clients can dereference either via `GET /api/v1/cases/{case_id}/processes/{process_id}/provenance` (ADR 0082 D-5).

instance.description: Post-migration workflow-process projection. Carries the new `workflowVersion` (and `workflowUrl` when changed).

migratedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp at which the migration committed (ADR 0069). Drawn from the server clock.

instanceMigratedRecordId.description: URN of the `instanceMigrated` Facts-tier provenance record (Kernel S11.3).

migrationPinChangedRecordId.description: URN of the `migrationPinChanged` Facts-tier provenance record (ADR 0071 D-4). Anchors the cross-layer chain transition for offline verifier reconstruction.

previousWorkflowVersion.description: Workflow Document version the instance was on immediately before this migration (Kernel S9.6).

newWorkflowVersion.description: Workflow Document version the instance is on immediately after this migration.

previousWorkflowUrl.description: Workflow Document URI the instance was on immediately before this migration. Omitted when the migration stayed on the same URL (the common in-place version-bump case).

newWorkflowUrl.description: Workflow Document URI the instance is on immediately after this migration. Omitted when the migration stayed on the same URL.
